description: [TODO] Describe what Mcp Server Bridge does

library_notes.general: Single responsibility - one widget does one thing. No global state. No project-specific code, hardcoded paths, credentials, or environment-specific values. Expose variation points as constructor parameters or function arguments - no hardcoded thresholds, colors, sizes, or behavior flags. URLs and endpoints belong in examples as demonstration data, not in src/ - if a widget needs a URL to operate, it must be a parameter the caller provides. All dependencies must be declared in widget.json tech_stack.dependencies. Widgets cannot depend on other widgets - they must be fully self-contained. If you need logic from another widget, copy it into src/ directly. Examples must run and exit cleanly with no user input, no network calls, and no external services or API keys - use fake/hardcoded data. The widget's own declared dependencies are fine and will be installed by the validator before the example runs. Do not modify library_notes - it is managed by Cartograph and will be restored on checkin.
library_notes.language: No project-specific names, identifiers, or terminology anywhere in src/, tests/, or examples/ - use generic names like 'user', 'item', 'value', 'example_org'. A test that references your project's real entity names is contaminated. Use pytest for all tests - no unittest. Type hints required on all public functions. No __init__.py in tests/. No if __name__ == '__main__' blocks in src/ files. Imports ordered: stdlib, third-party, local. ML domain: framework-free (numpy, scikit-learn, plain Python) and API-based inference (HTTP calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, HuggingFace Inference API) work everywhere. Heavy frameworks (torch, tensorflow, jax, and similar) are allowed but must be pre-installed - Cartograph will not auto-install them. Declare them in widget.json dependencies normally; consumers must also have them installed to validate.
library_notes.domain: Every tunable should be a parameter: timeouts, retry counts, pool sizes, base URLs, header names. A consumer must be able to point your widget at any endpoint or database without touching source. Never hardcode URLs in src/ - accept them as parameters and demonstrate with a real URL in examples/. Return structured data (dicts, typed objects) rather than formatted strings - let the caller decide how to present it. Do not log to stdout/stderr; raise exceptions or return error values instead.
